content: Just as the medial prefrontal cortex-hippocampus interaction in human memory retrieval coordinates rapid pattern association of stored memory traces and gradual stabilisation of relevant contextual relationships to pull out targeted memories, LightRAG’s graph-based retrieval architecture can be redesigned to mimic this two-phase biological interaction to improve long-document knowledge retrieval. This re-engineered LightRAG would separate the roles of its two core components: one replicating the hippocampus’s function of rapidly indexing and associating scattered contextual knowledge fragments from indexed documents, while the other replicates the medial prefrontal cortex’s role of guiding targeted retrieval and gradually stabilising semantically consistent query-relevant knowledge subgraphs to reduce irrelevant context hallucinations.
